steps.title: Spot the explicit contrast
steps.content: The passage explicitly identifies different Indigenous language sources for each word—Nahuatl for cocoa, Quechua for alpaca.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B names these exact language sources: Nahuatl for cocoa and Quechua for alpaca.

The other three introduce claims unsupported by the passage: when the words entered English (A), meaning changes during borrowing (C), and public misconceptions about origins (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
